title: #eng-platform: 1769984123
preview:
Jada Williams: Kicking off the spike thread. We need to decide *where* to hook SLO evaluation into the rollout state machine. Question: do we evaluate inside the controller loop (before advancing steps) or as an async sidecar / separate worker that can pause/rollback via API?

Chloe Martin: +1. From Console side we ...
text:
Jada Williams: Kicking off the spike thread. We need to decide *where* to hook SLO evaluation into the rollout state machine. Question: do we evaluate inside the controller loop (before advancing steps) or as an async sidecar / separate worker that can pause/rollback via API?

Chloe Martin: +1. From Console side we mainly need consistent semantics + auditability. But for the actual hook, I’d prefer controller-owned so it’s deterministic w/ step transitions.

Ethan Park: Control plane POV: if controller owns evaluation, it must not block step transitions on slow monitor queries. We’ll need timeouts + “monitor missing/stale” behavior spelled out.

Benji Okafor: Also policy engine needs to supply defaults/override per rollout. If evaluation happens in controller, it needs to fetch a resolved policy bundle at runtime (and handle policy versioning).

Jordan Lee: State machine option: evalu...

title: #eng-platform: 1769536063
preview:
Ethan Park: quick q for folks thinking about Console integration: should the cost-model API be called synchronously on the request path, or should Console only consume async aggregates (dashboards) and keep request-path stuff out of it?

Chloe Martin: for Console, the cost breakdown page is effectively “hot path” wh...
text:
Ethan Park: quick q for folks thinking about Console integration: should the cost-model API be called synchronously on the request path, or should Console only consume async aggregates (dashboards) and keep request-path stuff out of it?

Chloe Martin: for Console, the cost breakdown page is effectively “hot path” when customers click around, but not *the* inference request path. still needs to be snappy (sub-second) and reliable.

Thomas Baird: +1 to being careful. if we make the API do heavy aggregation synchronously over high-cardinality telemetry (filters + group-bys) we’ll get cardinality blowups and p95 will go sideways.

Benji Okafor: I think we’re mixing 2 use cases:
1) per-request estimate/breakdown (bounded inputs, deterministic)
2) time-window aggregates for dashboards (potentially huge)
We can do (1) sync. (2) should be async or pre-aggregated.

Jada Williams: Console pages...

text:
Nadia Rahman: hey folks — working through alerting for the marketplace entitlement sync job (post PR-19878). can we align on thresholds + what should page vs just warn? also need a quick dashboard spec for partnerships/support.

Cole Summers: yep. we should separate “job is broken” from “external propagation is slow”. job-broken should page.

Ethan Park: +1. i’d page on: no successful syncs for X mins, or sync loop crashing, or backlog not draining.

Nadia Rahman: current metrics we have in staging:
- `entitlement_sync_run_success_total{provider}` / `..._failure_total{provider,reason}`
- `entitlement_sync_loop_duration_seconds{provider}`
- `entitlement_sync_backlog_items{provider}` (queue length)
- `entitlement_sync_oldest_item_age_seconds{provider}`
- `entitlement_sync_last_success_timestamp_seconds{provider}`
- `entitlement_provisioning_time_seconds{provider}` (subscription seen -> ...

title: #eng-platform: 1769388094
preview:
Confluence Bot: ADR updated: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-022+Cost+Breakdown+Taxonomy (page changed by Ethan Park)

Ethan Park: hey folks, last call on ADR-022 wording. biggest open item is the “overhead allocation” definition + how it shows up in breakdown. pls drop any final comments he...
text:
Confluence Bot: ADR updated: https://confluence.redwoodinference.com/display/ARCH/ADR-022+Cost+Breakdown+Taxonomy (page changed by Ethan Park)

Ethan Park: hey folks, last call on ADR-022 wording. biggest open item is the “overhead allocation” definition + how it shows up in breakdown. pls drop any final comments here so i can merge today.

Allison Grant: skimmed. taxonomy is good. one thing: the ADR currently uses “overhead” in 2 ways (platform shared + request-level overhead like queueing). recommend we reserve “overhead” for *shared/non-attributable* and call the other “request_execution_overhead” or similar.

Logan Wright: +1. telemetry already has “queue_time_ms” etc, we don’t want that to get lumped into Finance overhead.

Laura Bennett: yes, please do not label queueing/runtime inefficiencies as “overhead” in finance sense. overhead for FP&A is “shared costs not attributable to...

title: #eng-platform: 1769196320
preview:
Ethan Park: quick q for Console/Optimize folks: we need to lock down schema version negotiation behavior for Cost Model API. Specifically: what should clients do if they see unknown fields / unknown enum values in responses, and what should server do if it receives newer fields in requests?

Chloe Martin: for Consol...
text:
Ethan Park: quick q for Console/Optimize folks: we need to lock down schema version negotiation behavior for Cost Model API. Specifically: what should clients do if they see unknown fields / unknown enum values in responses, and what should server do if it receives newer fields in requests?

Chloe Martin: for Console, biggest risk is the cost breakdown UI exploding if we suddenly get a new `breakdown.*` bucket we don’t understand. we can ignore unknown but we need a place to surface it (maybe "Other" bucket?) so totals still match.

Jada Williams: +1. also, for typed parsing we should confirm how we’ll handle unknown enum values. if we use JSON + codegen, unknown enums can deserialize as 0 / default and that’s super dangerous.

Aditya Rao: Optimize side: we’re fine to ignore unknown response fields but we need to preserve them when we proxy/store results (for debugging). also our what...

title: #eng-platform: 1768921186
preview:
Ethan Park: quick perf review on cost-model endpoint before we widen Console flag. seeing p95 spike when Console loads the breakdown table (multi-series). can folks drop observations / ideas? esp caching + cardinality.

Thomas Baird: pulled stats from staging + a couple prod shadow queries. the hot path is `/v1/cost...
text:
Ethan Park: quick perf review on cost-model endpoint before we widen Console flag. seeing p95 spike when Console loads the breakdown table (multi-series). can folks drop observations / ideas? esp caching + cardinality.

Thomas Baird: pulled stats from staging + a couple prod shadow queries. the hot path is `/v1/cost/estimate:batch` (Console calls it once per page w/ ~50 request summaries).

Thomas Baird: numbers from prod-canary (last 2h):
```
endpoint: cost-estimate-batch
p50: 42ms
p95: 410ms
p99: 1.8s
cpu: 75-95% on 2 pods
alloc: ~2.2GB/min
worst offenders: requests w/ breakdown_dimensions=[model,route,region,cache_state,quant_profile,customer_tier]
```

Benji Okafor: :eyes: 1.8s p99 is too spicy for Console.

Allison Grant: do we know if this is compute or waiting on downstream (telemetry store / model catalog)?

Thomas Baird: breakdown: ~55-65% compute (grouping + normalization), ...

text:
Ethan Park: hey folks — looking for reviewers on the cost model schema PR. mostly types + docs + serialization tests.

PR: https://github.com/redwood/redwood/pull/28451

Would love :eyes: from @Allison Grant (api standards) + @Jada Williams/@Chloe Martin (Console) + @Logan Wright/@Nadia Rahman (telemetry) + @Kaitlyn Nguyen (versioning/auth implications).

2 specific questions before I merge:
1) naming conventions: we’re using `snake_case` JSON keys in the schema docs + generated types. any objections / places where we should align with existing metering names?
2) deprecation policy: for v1, do we want an explicit “deprecated fields” mechanism (docs + lint) vs just “don’t remove/rename, only add”?

GitHub Bot: Ethan Park opened pull request #28451: "Add cost-model schema package v1"

title: #eng-platform: 1768697002
preview:
Logan Wright: pulling folks in here re perf-canary control plane config surface. we need to stop hand-editing helm values per region 🙃

Logan Wright: goal: model list + thresholds + dims (region/tier/SKU) defined centrally, validated, and fully audited.

Noah Patel: +1. right now we have canary config in the perf-c...
text:
Logan Wright: pulling folks in here re perf-canary control plane config surface. we need to stop hand-editing helm values per region 🙃

Logan Wright: goal: model list + thresholds + dims (region/tier/SKU) defined centrally, validated, and fully audited.

Noah Patel: +1. right now we have canary config in the perf-canary repo and we’re going to end up with “who changed the thresholds?” incidents.

Nadia Rahman: can you clarify what exactly lives in control plane vs baseline store? baseline artifacts are big (histograms etc). config should just reference baseline ids.

Logan Wright: yep. my proposal:
- Control plane stores *policy/config*: target sets, per-model thresholds, min-samples, dampening, etc.
- Baseline store (object storage) stores *baseline stats/artifacts*, versioned, addressed by `baseline_ref`.

Caleb Johnson: please keep telemetry label dimensions aligned w whatever CP ...

title: #eng-platform: 1768660200
preview:
Benji Okafor: hey all — trying to lock feature-flag strategy for failover controller rollout (per tier/tenant). what’s the cleanest plan that gives us (1) staged enablement and (2) hard guardrails tied to metrics?

Mina Haddad: +1, we need something routing can reason about deterministically. “enabled for prod” isn’...
text:
Benji Okafor: hey all — trying to lock feature-flag strategy for failover controller rollout (per tier/tenant). what’s the cleanest plan that gives us (1) staged enablement and (2) hard guardrails tied to metrics?

Mina Haddad: +1, we need something routing can reason about deterministically. “enabled for prod” isn’t enough; we want to pin by tenant/tier and be able to yank quickly.

Ravi Menon: agree. also reminder: the controller touches policy flips, so any rollout needs anti-flap + rate limiting (ADR-043). if we do per-tenant, we have to avoid config drift / “mystery allowlist”.

Jordan Lee: from policy-engine side, I’d prefer a small set of flags with explicit precedence:
1) global kill switch
2) environment gate (staging/prod)
3) execution mode (off | dry_run | execute)
4) scope (tier allowlist + tenant allowlist)
we can implement as config in control plane + cached.

Miles Thor...

title: #eng-platform: 1768603310
preview:
Jada Williams: quick gut check on feature flag cohort assignment for lifecycle growth experiments (email + console). we need deterministic cohorts + a clean holdout group. what’s the approach we’re going with?

Ethan Park: proposal: assign at **workspace/org** level (not user) using a stable hash on `org_id` + `expe...
text:
Jada Williams: quick gut check on feature flag cohort assignment for lifecycle growth experiments (email + console). we need deterministic cohorts + a clean holdout group. what’s the approach we’re going with?

Ethan Park: proposal: assign at **workspace/org** level (not user) using a stable hash on `org_id` + `experiment_key` + a salt.
- output bucket 0-99
- bucket ranges map to variants (e.g., 0-44 = treatment_a, 45-89 = treatment_b, 90-99 = holdout)
- stickiness guaranteed bc hash is deterministic

Benji Okafor: +1 org-level. if we do user-level we’ll end up with mixed experiences inside the same team (esp console nudges). also makes support “why did my teammate see X” harder.

Logan Wright: from analytics side: please make sure we emit a single canonical property for every relevant event (signup, api_key_created, first_request, 100k_tokens) like:
- `exp_lifecycle_growth_v1` = `hol...

WARN toolcall_truncate req=01F7 tool=search trunc_after=1024 reason=timeout
INFO stream_resume req=01F7 route=fp model=redwood-fp-v1 retry=1
INFO billing provisional_charge tokens=1532
INFO billing credit_adjustment tokens=-1532 reason=partial_tool_trunc```

text:
Dylan Brooks: trying to nail down Console surface area for batching SLO tiers. question: where do we configure tier (fast/standard/cost-efficient) so it matches policy precedence and doesn’t confuse customers?

Nikhil Sharma: +1. Hosted API wants: if you do nothing, you get a sane default. but also want customers to see what they’re actually getting.

Aditya Rao: Optimize POV: Console should show “Recommended tier” + “Effective tier”. recommendation can be a hint, effective is what’s enforced.

Chloe Martin: we currently have Route Policy editor (per endpoint) where you can pick model + params. adding a “Latency/Cost tier” dropdown there seems straightforward. but what about per-project default / org default?

Priya Natarajan: careful: control plane schema is route policy > project default > platform default. Dedicated also adds tenant override. Console needs to represent that stack w...

text:
Dylan Brooks: pulling folks in here re: activation funnel instrumentation. we need to lock event taxonomy + required props + dedupe logic today so console + api teams aren’t implementing diff versions. current funnel is signup -> api key -> first request -> first 100k tokens within 7d.

Nadia Rahman: yep. biggest issue rn is 1) inconsistent event names between console + api, 2) duplicates (especially signup), 3) missing join keys (org_id / workspace_id) so Mei can’t build clean funnels.

Logan Wright: +1. also: we should decide what’s authoritative for “first_successful_request”. if SDK emits it, we’ll get false positives / retries. best is server-side from API gateway/service.

Dylan Brooks: agree. console can emit ui events (api key created button click etc) but anything “request succeeded” should be server.

title: #eng-platform: 1768466901
preview:
Jordan Lee: got a few Qs on deterministic eval semantics for ADR-042 before we lock the policy engine config schema.

Jordan Lee: 1) When multiple signals are "bad" at the same time, are we strictly priority-ordered (first match wins), or do we compute some combined score/confidence?

Jordan Lee: 2) For corroboratio...
text:
Jordan Lee: got a few Qs on deterministic eval semantics for ADR-042 before we lock the policy engine config schema.

Jordan Lee: 1) When multiple signals are "bad" at the same time, are we strictly priority-ordered (first match wins), or do we compute some combined score/confidence?

Jordan Lee: 2) For corroboration: is that expressed in the config (e.g., reachability requires >=N probes failing), or is it hardcoded per signal type?

Jordan Lee: 3) Missing signals: if capacity feed is delayed / nil, do we treat as unknown or good? trying to avoid NPEs + avoid accidentally suppressing failover.

Jordan Lee: Draft schema idea (rough):
```yaml
failover:
 evaluation_mode: deterministic
 hierarchy:
 - reason: REACHABILITY
 when:
 probe_fail_rate: ">=0.6"
 window: 60s
 action: FAILOVER
 - reason: ERROR_BURN
 when:
 five_xx_burn_rate: ">=14x"
 window: 5m
 corroborate:
 - tail_latency_burn_r...

title: #eng-platform: 1768427740
preview:
Ethan Park: trying to unblock the Cost Model API shape question for v1. do we want Console to *pull* (sync request -> cost breakdown), or do we want a *push* model (emit events + precomputed aggregates in a store) and Console reads from that?

Ethan Park: constraints: Console hot paths need p95 < ~150ms end-to-end f...
text:
Ethan Park: trying to unblock the Cost Model API shape question for v1. do we want Console to *pull* (sync request -> cost breakdown), or do we want a *push* model (emit events + precomputed aggregates in a store) and Console reads from that?

Ethan Park: constraints: Console hot paths need p95 < ~150ms end-to-end for the cost panel. also we cannot let this endpoint become a cardinality bomb.

Thomas Baird: push is the only way you’re gonna hit p95 reliably for “dashboards” use cases. if Console is rendering a table for 7d + group by route/model/customer it’s basically an analytics query.

Chloe Martin: +1 on perf concerns, but Console also has “request details” view (per request / trace) where we want to show breakdown for *that* request. that’s not really a push/agg thing.

Benji Okafor: we might be mixing 2 things:
1) estimate/breakdown for a single request or small batch -> synchr...

title: #eng-platform: 1768123456
preview:
Ethan Park: quick follow-up from the recent postmortem — noticed recurring theme: ramps tuned for throughput, not failure isolation. want a small set of defensible controls we can ship quickly.
Zoe Alvarez: yep. suggestion: signature-based rollback (identify regression fingerprints) + probabilistic canary sampling s...
text:
Ethan Park: quick follow-up from the recent postmortem — noticed recurring theme: ramps tuned for throughput, not failure isolation. want a small set of defensible controls we can ship quickly.
Zoe Alvarez: yep. suggestion: signature-based rollback (identify regression fingerprints) + probabilistic canary sampling so we don't blast the entire cohort. keep it simple to start.
Kai Thompson: signature rollback sounds interesting. basically we capture a small vector of metrics (error codes, top stack traces, product KPI delta) and if the vector matches a known regression pattern we automatically abort?
Priya Nair: exactly. and add a cooldown so we don't flip flags repeatedly. also add a human-in-loop tier for major-product-impact flags.
Ethan Park: proposed minimal defaults: initial_pct=2%, ramp minutes=20, probabilistic_sampling=true (sample 2% of users globally, then deterministic routi...

title: #eng-platform: 1768080411
preview:
Julian Moore: hey platform folks — quick q on config propagation for the KV compaction kill-switch. We need to know worst/typical latency from flipping the flag to every runtime worker actually observing it. Any gotchas w/ safe-disable semantics?

Julian Moore: context: runtime-1.22 canary. kill-switch should stop c...
text:
Julian Moore: hey platform folks — quick q on config propagation for the KV compaction kill-switch. We need to know worst/typical latency from flipping the flag to every runtime worker actually observing it. Any gotchas w/ safe-disable semantics?

Julian Moore: context: runtime-1.22 canary. kill-switch should stop compaction/eviction quickly if we see p99 blow up. we’re wiring it into scheduler loop, but control-plane propagation is the unknown.

Benji Okafor: yep. which path are you using? the standard runtime dynamic config (control-plane -> configd -> sidecar -> runtime), or LaunchDarkly-only?

Julian Moore: standard runtime dynamic config. we’re also gating behind FF for rollout, but the “oh crap” switch we want as close to immediate as possible.

Logan Wright: for dynamic config: control-plane publishes update within ~1–3s, configd long-polls w/ 10s max wait. typical node pickup ...

title: #eng-platform: 1767938855
preview:
Ethan Park: Heads up: SDK parity work is blocked on a couple gateway consistency things. Specifically:
- 429s need consistent `Retry-After` (present on *all* 429s, incl OpenAI-compat paths)
- error body needs stable fields so SDKs can classify (code, request_id, status)
- request_id needs to always be present on err...
text:
Ethan Park: Heads up: SDK parity work is blocked on a couple gateway consistency things. Specifically:
- 429s need consistent `Retry-After` (present on *all* 429s, incl OpenAI-compat paths)
- error body needs stable fields so SDKs can classify (code, request_id, status)
- request_id needs to always be present on errors (streaming + non-streaming)

We have PRs queued on SDK side that assume these. Can we align on what the gateway will emit + timeline?

Ibrahim Khan: +1. Conformance tests are failing on a simulated 429 where Python/TS parse Retry-After but gateway doesn’t always send it today. We’re standardizing SDK behavior to:
- if 429 and Retry-After present -> sleep that long (bounded)
- if missing -> exponential backoff + jitter

So gateway consistency is the best path.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: For error fields: please confirm we’re not including any auth context or tokens anywhere (heade...

title: #eng-platform: 1764021507
preview:
Ethan Park: heads up — planning to roll out the owner-mapping config update today (control plane config: incident_type/subtype -> owner + secondary + runbook links). this is the one that fixes the Routing vs Fleet split for capacity subtypes + adds a couple missing subtypes for Private connectivity.

text:
Ethan Park: heads up — planning to roll out the owner-mapping config update today (control plane config: incident_type/subtype -> owner + secondary + runbook links). this is the one that fixes the Routing vs Fleet split for capacity subtypes + adds a couple missing subtypes for Private connectivity.

Ethan Park: proposal is a prod canary first, then full rollout if metrics are clean. can someone sanity check the canary + monitoring checklist below?

Dylan Brooks: yep. what’s the blast radius here again — only incident-bot owner resolution + pager routing that consumes the mapping, right? no serving path.

Ethan Park: correct. no request path. consumers:
- incident-bot modal validation + auto-owner + runbook link suggestions
- pager routing helper that tries to page primary owner when `incident_type` is set
- console ops view (read-only) showing resolved owner

Allison Grant: + metrics...

title: #eng-platform: 1764017782
preview:
Ethan Park: heads up: Console needs to show quality gate results + artifact links for Optimize recommendations. Can we align on what the Console API needs from Optimize service vs a separate artifacts endpoint?

Chloe Martin: yup, we’re building the Console backend slice now. Need an explicit contract from Optimize....
text:
Ethan Park: heads up: Console needs to show quality gate results + artifact links for Optimize recommendations. Can we align on what the Console API needs from Optimize service vs a separate artifacts endpoint?

Chloe Martin: yup, we’re building the Console backend slice now. Need an explicit contract from Optimize. Biggest questions: (1) what IDs we get back, (2) how to fetch artifacts securely, (3) what’s included in the “summary” vs “details”.

Dylan Brooks: +1. UX wants a compact banner in the recommendation drawer + a drilldown page with comparisons. We can’t render if we only have a single pass/fail boolean.

Yara Haddad: from Optimize side we already store gate runs (state machine). Can return `gate_run_id` + status and some rollup metrics. For artifacts we currently have S3 object keys + eval-harness run id.

Benji Okafor: request: please don’t leak raw S3 keys to Console clie...

title: #eng-platform: 1763764440
preview:
Sergio Costa: need 15 min of alignment on perf baseline dashboards. main goal: dashboard -> specific run manifest -> traces w/ zero guessing. what fields do we standardize + what’s the run naming convention?

Allison Grant: +1. if we don’t lock tags now we’ll have 10 near-duplicates in grafana. propose we treat run ...
text:
Sergio Costa: need 15 min of alignment on perf baseline dashboards. main goal: dashboard -> specific run manifest -> traces w/ zero guessing. what fields do we standardize + what’s the run naming convention?

Allison Grant: +1. if we don’t lock tags now we’ll have 10 near-duplicates in grafana. propose we treat run manifest as source of truth and dashboards only reflect manifest fields.

Rina Kobayashi: for the harness we can output whatever, but we need a convention that’s stable + parseable. also: engineers are already copy/pasting “run names” into slack, so it should be human-readable.

Logan Wright: from telemetry side, easiest if every metric series has a `run_id` (uuid) AND a `run_slug` (human). uuid for joins, slug for eyeballing.

Sean Gallagher: also for oncall, when we get an alert we need a one-click link to the exact manifest + trace. “which run was this?” should not be a ...

title: #eng-platform: 1763408120

text:
Dylan Brooks: seeing a weird owner-resolution bug in incident-bot. subtype `capacity_fleet.provisioning_lag` is resolving owner as `@eng-platform-routing` instead of `@eng-infra-fleet`.

Dylan Brooks: repro: `/incident create` -> select Incident Type: Capacity/Fleet -> Subtype: Provisioning lag -> modal shows Owner: Routing (Smart Routing). expected Fleet.

Devon Price: yep, can repro in #incidents too. also the runbook link suggestion is the routing runbook, not the fleet capacity one.

incident-bot: Owner resolution details
- incident_type: capacity_fleet
- incident_subtype: provisioning_lag
- resolved_owner: eng-platform-routing
- mapping_source: control-plane (owner_mapping v7)

Ethan Park: thanks. sounds like a bad entry in the owner-mapping config (or fallback logic picking wrong default). can someone paste the current mapping row for that subtype?

Devon Price: from `owner_mapp...

title: #eng-platform: 1763004555
preview: Benji Okafor: we keep getting on-call asks for “why did this request get 429’d / shed” and we’re stuck piecing it from logs + dashboards. proposing an internal debug endpoint in routing/policy eval path that returns the admission decision + reason + budgets snapshot. thoughts on security/access control before i open...
text:
Benji Okafor: we keep getting on-call asks for “why did this request get 429’d / shed” and we’re stuck piecing it from logs + dashboards. proposing an internal debug endpoint in routing/policy eval path that returns the admission decision + reason + budgets snapshot. thoughts on security/access control before i open PR?

Jordan Lee: +1 on having a deterministic view. but we need to be really careful about what we return. policy eval includes tenant tier, route group, sometimes account allowlists, and capacity signals. easy to leak cross-tenant info if we’re not strict.

Kaitlyn Nguyen: what service are you thinking hosts it? gateway vs routing vs policy-engine?

Benji Okafor: leaning routing service because it already has request context (route group, region, model pinning) and calls policy-engine. endpoint would take request_id OR a synthetic “dry-run” input.

Ravi Menon: dry-run inp...

title: #eng-platform: 1762738801
preview: Benji Okafor: can someone sanity check the canary rollout plan for hot-route protection? esp allowlisted accounts + rollback conditions + config migration risk. thinking us-east only first, 5 enterprise accts, chat+embeddings route groups. link to checklist draft: https://drive.google.com/redwood/launch-checklist-ho...
text:
Benji Okafor: can someone sanity check the canary rollout plan for hot-route protection? esp allowlisted accounts + rollback conditions + config migration risk. thinking us-east only first, 5 enterprise accts, chat+embeddings route groups. link to checklist draft: https://drive.google.com/redwood/launch-checklist-hot-route

Ethan Park: yup. what’s the proposed allowlist? and are we gating at gateway flag or policy-engine flag (or both)?

Benji Okafor: both. gateway enforces tier-aware limiter behind `hot_route_protection_gateway` and routing/policy behind `hot_route_protection_policy`. canary is allowlist at account_id in config service.

Jordan Lee: +1 on dual gating. if policy eval changes are on but gateway off, we still risk “silent” admission behavior changes. i’d prefer: policy + routing in shadow mode for 24h, then flip gateway enforcement for allowlist.

Amara Diallo: on gatew...

title: #eng-platform: 1761123456
preview:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — infra needs a concrete list of KV / prefix-cache signals for the Console dashboards and routing policy UX. Can runtime teams confirm what we can realistically emit today?

Ravi Patel: We can emit hit/miss counters, eviction counters, and a reuse counter scoped to (tenant, model, route). Need t...
text:
Ava Chen: Quick sync — infra needs a concrete list of KV / prefix-cache signals for the Console dashboards and routing policy UX. Can runtime teams confirm what we can realistically emit today?

Ravi Patel: We can emit hit/miss counters, eviction counters, and a reuse counter scoped to (tenant, model, route). Need to agree on label cardinality.

Juno Park: Agree on labels. Proposal: tenant_hash, model, route, seq_len_bucket (0-128,129-512,>512), workload_tag. Keep tenant hashed to avoid PII.

Eli Morales: For prefix cache specifically we also have prefix_reuse_count and prefix_reuse_window_ms — useful to surface reuse vs short-lived prefixes.

Ava Chen: Good. Console product asks for hit_rate and eviction_rate (ratio) and a per-workload "reuse by workload" bar/heatmap. Also a small table of top N tenants by eviction spikes. Privacy ok with hashed tenant?

Fran López: hashed tenant_id ...

title: #eng-platform: 1760965400
preview:
Dylan Brooks: We keep getting support asking for “what bundle did you install + did verify pass” and it’s not in the support bundle today. Can we add offline bundle metadata + signature verification output to the support bundle artifact? (for air-gapped / Private)

Ethan Park: +1. Right now we have to ask customers ...
text:
Dylan Brooks: We keep getting support asking for “what bundle did you install + did verify pass” and it’s not in the support bundle today. Can we add offline bundle metadata + signature verification output to the support bundle artifact? (for air-gapped / Private)

Ethan Park: +1. Right now we have to ask customers to rerun preflight with `--verbose` and paste logs.

Logan Wright: From telemetry side: we already emit local audit events for verify success/failure, but support bundle is the right place to snapshot it. What exactly do we want included?

Dev Patel: Support POV: minimum is (1) bundle build id/version, (2) manifest hash, (3) signature verify status + failure reason/error code, (4) signing key id / fingerprint that was used, and (5) whether break-glass was used.

Owen Phillips: Also the *path* to the bundle they pointed the installer at. Half of the time it’s the wrong tarba...

title: #eng-platform: 1759910223
preview:
Allison Grant: trying to unblock status page v2 rollout: dashboards + alerts naming is all over the place rn. we need a consistent mapping from internal service names -> public status components so oncall can pick the right component quickly.

Aisha Bello: +1. the SLO burn alerts i’m wiring up are labeled `service=g...
text:
Allison Grant: trying to unblock status page v2 rollout: dashboards + alerts naming is all over the place rn. we need a consistent mapping from internal service names -> public status components so oncall can pick the right component quickly.

Aisha Bello: +1. the SLO burn alerts i’m wiring up are labeled `service=gateway` / `cluster=use1` / etc. but status page wants `component_id` + `region` + maybe `capability` (streaming/embeddings).

Logan Wright: i can take a pass at the mapping table, but we need to agree on the “public label” vocabulary. do we already have canonical ids?

Christine Wong: taxonomy doc has draft ids, but we keep drifting in Grafana. example: some dashboards call it “API Gateway - US East” and others “Hosted API (use1)”.

Mina Haddad: canonical should be: product surface x region x capability. region tokens should match routing taxonomy: `us-east`, `us-west`, `eu...

title: #eng-platform: 1759004321
preview:
Alex Chen: Quick sync — planning the /api/generate shim cutover next Thursday (TZ: PST). Owners: Maria (SDK), Samir (runtime), Jordan (docs). Any blockers?
Maria Lopez: I have PR 4523 open for the SDK header compatibility. Link: https://git.company/repo/pull/4523. Tests failing in CI because of an auth header shape ...
text:
Alex Chen: Quick sync — planning the /api/generate shim cutover next Thursday (TZ: PST). Owners: Maria (SDK), Samir (runtime), Jordan (docs). Any blockers?
Maria Lopez: I have PR 4523 open for the SDK header compatibility. Link: https://git.company/repo/pull/4523. Tests failing in CI because of an auth header shape change.
Samir Patel: Noted. I'm seeing a perf regression on long-context requests with the new kernel selection, likely unrelated to the header but will triage. ETA tomorrow EOD.
Jordan Blake: Docs draft is here: https://docs.company/springboard/shim-cutover. Need final callouts on fallback behavior and example code.
Alex Chen: Maria — how much work to make SDK accept both header forms for a short backcompat window?
Maria Lopez: Quick patch is ~2 files, ~45 LOC. I can push a short-lived shim that accepts both and logs a deprecation warning. Will add an env flag to flip off....

title: #eng-platform: 1758019999
preview:
Ava Chen: quick late drop-in — routing policy PR merged but staging shows intermittent 401s after jwt rotation. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/routing/pull/482 :wave:
Samir Patel: saw the 401 spike around 10:12. stacktrace points to the proxy dropping `kid` when header is forwarded through the edge. adding...
text:
Ava Chen: quick late drop-in — routing policy PR merged but staging shows intermittent 401s after jwt rotation. PR: https://github.com/redwood-inference/routing/pull/482 :wave:
Samir Patel: saw the 401 spike around 10:12. stacktrace points to the proxy dropping `kid` when header is forwarded through the edge. adding logs now.
Maya Ortiz: pinging @Theo Li — did we change anything in the jwt middleware for staging yesterday?
Theo Li: I deployed the rotation job but didn't touch middleware. I'll take a look at proxy headers in 20m. Can you paste an example failing header?
Samir Patel: here's a sanitized example: `Authorization: Bearer eyJhbGciOiJSUzI1NiIsInR5cCI6IkpXVCIsImtpZCI6ImVjMiJ9...` The `kid` is present in the token header but downstream service sees empty kid.
Ava Chen: repro steps I used: `curl -H "Authorization: Bearer <token>" https://staging-gw.redwood/api/health` got 401. O...

title: #eng-platform: 1755004327
preview:
Alex Kim: Quick follow-up to the RFC — proposal to move 'circuit-breaker' decision from router to observer service and make router do only routing+fallback execution. Thoughts?

Priya Singh: curious — what's the motivation? operationally it's easier to centralize decisions, but we lose local fast-fail ability.

Alex...
text:
Alex Kim: Quick follow-up to the RFC — proposal to move 'circuit-breaker' decision from router to observer service and make router do only routing+fallback execution. Thoughts?

Priya Singh: curious — what's the motivation? operationally it's easier to centralize decisions, but we lose local fast-fail ability.

Alex Kim: Main reasons: 1) observer has aggregated cross-model signals (error-rate spikes, latency p50/p95, token-queue-growth) to make more informed degrade decisions; 2) easier to share safety policies across hosted/dedicated/private. Router still needs a fast local check (cacheable) for immediate O(1) rejects.

Ben Carter: Do we have an API surface sketched? e.g., router calls observer.isHealthy(model, tenant, cohort) sync -> bool? That could add latency.

Jia Li: maybe make it async: router consults cached verdicts (ttl 5s) + probe hook to observer for stale entries. If cac...

title: #eng-platform: 1749991111
preview:
Priya Desai: FYI customer hit a billing surprise when a streaming generation was truncated by server-side max_tokens enforcement mid-toolcall and the client retried. Result: provisional reserve from original stream + confirmed smaller final = weird net. anyone seen truncation + retry double-charge?

Ethan Park: can ...
text:
Priya Desai: FYI customer hit a billing surprise when a streaming generation was truncated by server-side max_tokens enforcement mid-toolcall and the client retried. Result: provisional reserve from original stream + confirmed smaller final = weird net. anyone seen truncation + retry double-charge?

Ethan Park: can you sketch the flow quickly? truncation during toolcall sounds nasty.

Priya Desai: high level: client starts stream -> server marks provisional 120 tokens when toolcall begins -> server enforces max_tokens and truncates output at 80 tokens, responds with truncated toolcall_confirm(80) -> client perceives truncated response and retries the toolcall -> replay causes another provisional 80 then confirm 80. end state: provisional 120 + confirmed 160 in ledger -> customer sees overage.

Riley Chen: ack. that's different from a reconnect; truncation caused provisional and confir...

title: #eng-platform: 1749472201
preview:
Nina Petrova: looping in eng-platform folks — we need to nail down the integration points between the new VPC terraform modules and platform/installer config. Today we’re doing a lot of “paste subnet ids here” in pilots and it’s brittle.

Victor Alvarez: +1. main question from my side: what outputs do we want to be ...
text:
Nina Petrova: looping in eng-platform folks — we need to nail down the integration points between the new VPC terraform modules and platform/installer config. Today we’re doing a lot of “paste subnet ids here” in pilots and it’s brittle.

Victor Alvarez: +1. main question from my side: what outputs do we want to be part of the “contract” for alpha? if we change names/shape every PR it’ll be chaos.

Irene Choi: yep. for alpha I’d like a minimal stable set that redwood-private-installer can consume (either flags or a config file). optional extras can be “best effort” outputs.

Bruno Silva: current modules output the basics (vpc_id, private_subnet_ids, public_subnet_ids, nat_gw_ids) but we haven’t standardized endpoint outputs. also log sinks.

Sean Gallagher: from SRE standpoint: the only things we *must* know to validate networking before we start are: subnets for nodes, cluster SGs, a...

title: #eng-platform: 1749256629
preview:
Logan Wright: Heads up: marketing site needs to pull benchmark results JSON for the interactive table. We have the internal results endpoint in `redwood` behind auth, but we need to settle: caching strategy + auth model + rate limiting so we don’t get scraped to death on launch week.

Felix Schneider: +1. Current pl...
text:
Logan Wright: Heads up: marketing site needs to pull benchmark results JSON for the interactive table. We have the internal results endpoint in `redwood` behind auth, but we need to settle: caching strategy + auth model + rate limiting so we don’t get scraped to death on launch week.

Felix Schneider: +1. Current plan in DES/PRD assumes a static-ish JSON artifact per report version (ex: `v1.0.0`) + optional “latest” pointer. If we call the internal endpoint from the client, we’ll leak too much + hammer origin.

Henry Cho: product POV: we should treat results as public once published, but we can’t expose internal IDs / infra details (region shard names, fleet tags, etc). Also want “last known good” behavior if we republish.

Mei Lin: data pipeline exports a versioned `results.json` already (from benchmark-lab). It has schema version + report_version + per-model/region stats. We can pub...
